sg_weights._note: TPC River Highlands: Par-70, 6820y, moderate difficulty. APP elevated (driver-wedge track, set-up holes demand precision). PUTT moderate (fast bentgrass at 12 stimp but larger greens). ARG reduced (limited recovery ops on short track). OTT standard (emphasis on distance over accuracy on short par-4s).

course_weight_override._note: Standard weights. TPC River Highlands rewards bombers and quality iron players. Increase courseFit to 0.24/0.19 if Phase 1 coverage > 50%.

course_history_note: The Travelers Championship is an annual event at TPC River Highlands. No fallback remapping needed — all historical rounds indexed under event_id=34.
winner_archetype: Aggressive ball-striker with power off the tee and quality wedge play. TPC River Highlands is a short par-70 where distance and birdie upside are rewarded. Recent winners: Rahm, Furyk, Spieth, Cantlay — players who attack pins and convert scoring opportunities. A strong short game and putting are essential on the fast bentgrass greens.
bbi_pipeline_action: FULL — post-major standard event. TPC River Highlands is a short, scoring-friendly par-70 with strong field. Standard weight calibrations apply.
course_notes: TPC River Highlands, Hartford CT. Par-70, 6820y. A Pete Dye design known for risk-reward golf strokes. Short but strategic with undulating bentgrass greens at 12 stimp. Favors bombers who can attack reachable par-5s and scoring par-4s. Distance off the tee is a premium; recovery around the greens is moderate importance. Historically low scoring. 4 drivable par-4s make approach play from 100-150 yards critical.
